text.title: ArgoCD GitOps Deployment Policy Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `ArgoCD GitOps Deployment Policy` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: CI MUST NOT run `kubectl apply` or `helm upgrade` against production. All cluster changes flow through Git.
rule.rationale: Direct CI access bypasses audit + self-heal; split-brain ensues.

rule.statement: Application source code and Kubernetes manifests MUST live in separate Git repos. CI updates the manifest repo after image push.
rule.rationale: Mixing repos couples build + deploy concerns and confuses access control.

rule.statement: Environment separation MUST use folders (`overlays/staging`, `overlays/production`), not Git branches.
rule.rationale: Branch-per-env makes promotion diffs hard; folder-per-env keeps diff side-by-side.

rule.statement: `syncOptions: [ServerSideApply=true]` MUST be set on production Applications to avoid field-manager conflicts.
rule.rationale: Client-side apply collides when multiple tools share resources.

rule.statement: Resource ordering MUST use `argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave` annotations: CRDs (-2), Namespaces (-1), Configs (0), StatefulSets (1), Deployments (2), Ingress (3).
rule.rationale: Without sync waves a Deployment can start before its ConfigMap exists, causing crash loops.

rule.statement: `automated.selfHeal: true` MUST be enabled for non-prod; production self-heal MUST be paired with a documented emergency-suspend procedure.
rule.rationale: Self-heal in prod can revert emergency patches; suspend procedure is the safety valve.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
